nodes.title: Wellness Trends Report 2026
nodes.content:
## Summary

Holland & Barrett's fifth annual Wellness Trends Report (published Dec 2025 by their global team of scientists, nutritionists, forecasters, and innovators) identifies five macro wellness shifts for 2026. The framing: "It's time to ditch the guilt trip. We're not lazy. We're hormonal, inflamed and overloaded." A science-backed shift is underway from willpower-focused wellness to understanding the biological and metabolic roots of health challenges.

The five macro trends: 1) Over Willpower (GLP-1 and metabolic science reshaping self-image); 2) Back Your Body Now (proactive, preventive health optimization replacing reactive care); 3) Where Nature Meets Next-Gen Science (bioengineered ingredients, precision fermentation); 4) Joy of Wellness (anti-optimization backlash; humor and color replacing sterile health aesthetics); 5) Community as wellness infrastructure (social wellness — run clubs, group retreats — as behavior change mechanism).

The GLP-1 section is particularly data-rich: H&B reports a near-doubling of UK GLP-1 household penetration (2.3% → 4.1%), and 77% of UK GLP-1 users have experienced at least one side effect — creating demand for supplementary nutrition support stacks.

## Key Claims

- Global wellness market projected at $8.99 trillion by 2028
- GLP-1 usage is forecast to reach 70M patients by 2030 globally (Morgan Stanley)
- UK GLP-1 household penetration nearly doubled: 2.3% → 4.1% (Kantar 2025)
- 77% of UK GLP-1 users experienced at least one side effect — creating demand for support supplementation
- GLP-1 households saw average -5.5% grocery bill reduction in first six months (Cornell, 2024)
- 72% of UK adults struggle to get through the day (energy crisis signal)
- Only 17% of UK adults meet 5-a-day fruit/vegetable recommendation; 96% miss daily fibre targets
- Global gut health market expected to surpass $90B by 2030
- AI wellness platforms projected to grow to $24B by 2030 — AI as personalized health coach
- 84% of Gen Z prioritize a healthy lifestyle over career, personal enjoyment, or money
- 1 in 4 consumers don't know how to look after their brain health at different life stages
- 68% of Gen Z say wellness feels more "doable" with community support
- Social media misinformation: only 2.1% of nutrition content on TikTok aligns with public health guidelines

## Data Points

- Global wellness market: $8.99T by 2028 (WGSN)
- GLP-1 global patients: 70M by 2030 (Morgan Stanley)
- UK household GLP-1 penetration: 2.3% → 4.1% (Kantar Worldpanel 2025)
- Gut health market: >$90B by 2030 (Markets & Markets)
- Brain health market: $15B by 2030 (Grand View Research)
- AI wellness platforms: $24B by 2030 (CB Insights)
- UK health check-up market: ~$4.42B by 2033 (Nova Advisor)
- Supplement stats: 2/3 Americans take supplements; 80% of Gen Z; 83% of $200K+ households

## Connections

- [[wiki/entities/glp-1-drugs]] — most detailed UK/European GLP-1 penetration data; side effects creating supplement opportunity
- [[wiki/concepts/wellness-optimization]] — primary source for wellness market size, personalization, AI health coaching
- [[wiki/concepts/coming-of-age]] — proactive aging, "back your body now" philosophy
- [[wiki/concepts/good-vibrations]] — Joy of Wellness trend; anti-optimization backlash; community wellness spaces

## Contradictions / Tensions

- Joy of Wellness (anti-optimization trend) directly contradicts the hyper-optimization trend seen in wearables and blood testing. Two consumer segments moving in opposite directions simultaneously.
- TikTok as the dominant wellness content platform (millions of views) while only 2.1% of TikTok nutrition content aligns with public health guidelines — entertainment vs. science tension

nodes.title: What's Trending 2026
nodes.content:
## Summary

BCD Meetings & Events' annual "What's Trending" report for 2026 provides a global MICE (Meetings, Incentives, Conferences, Events) industry outlook. The framing: "the true value of meetings and events lies in human connection." The report covers MICE trends by region (NORAM, UK, EMEA, APAC, LATAM) plus thematic sections on: destinations, meetings management, collective approaches, venue search evolution, and AI/tech adoption.

Three headline trends for MICE 2026: (1) AI-driven personalization transforming attendee engagement; (2) predictive analytics enabling smarter, more effective event formats; (3) sustainability becoming foundational with tools to measure and offset impact. BCD is explicit that meetings are increasingly treated as "strategic assets" with spending aligned more closely with business objectives — moving the MICE category from expense to investment.

The BCD/Minor Hotels partnership section reveals MICE's growing strategic importance: over a quarter of Minor Hotels' global revenue comes from MICE business, described as offering "rare stability, predictability and long-term value" compared to volatile B2C demand. This positions corporate events as a resilience strategy for hospitality operators.

## Key Claims

- Meetings increasingly treated as "strategic assets" aligned with business objectives, not discretionary events
- Three MICE trends for 2026: AI personalization, predictive analytics, sustainability measurement
- MICE represents >25% of Minor Hotels' global revenue — a strategic stability driver vs. B2C volatility
- AI-powered booking tools and streamlined processes are being deployed across the MICE stack
- Immersive experiences and thoughtful design are setting new quality standards
- Economic pressure, sustainability requirements, and geopolitical complexity are all influencing event decisions
- BCD's "Thinking Forward" three-year plan (launched 2024) is in its second year of execution
- Technology adoption in MICE is the "burning issue" flagged across all regional markets

## Data Points

- Publisher: BCD Meetings & Events (global corporate travel and meetings management company)
- Minor Hotels: MICE = >25% of global revenue
- Regional data included for NORAM, UK, EMEA, APAC, LATAM

## Connections

- [[wiki/concepts/agentic-ai-enterprise]] — AI-powered meeting personalization; predictive analytics for event management
- [[wiki/concepts/good-vibrations]] — human connection as the core value proposition; immersive shared experiences
- [[wiki/concepts/stability-premium]] — MICE as stable B2B revenue vs. B2C volatility; trust and sustainability credentials

## Contradictions / Tensions

- MICE industry advocates for in-person gatherings while AI tools are making remote collaboration more effective. The industry's ROI argument for meetings must get stronger as travel costs, sustainability concerns, and time pressure all favor digital alternatives.
